market_context: Futures are up over 1% on US-Iran ceasefire optimism despite Iran denying direct talks. Oil prices dropped 5-6% on diplomatic hopes. Private credit crisis at Apollo with major redemption gates is pressuring asset managers.

predictions.reasoning: S&P 500 futures are up over 1% on Iran ceasefire optimism, with contracts showing strong momentum. Energy sector weakness from falling oil prices should be offset by broader market relief rally from geopolitical tensions easing.

ARM announced its first in-house AGI CPU chip and jumped 15% pre-market. Raymond James upgraded to Outperform with $166 target citing business model transformation. Strong momentum with major tech partnerships including Meta and OpenAI.

Apollo faces severe redemption pressure with 11.2% withdrawal requests capped at 5%, creating liquidity crisis narrative. Private credit sector under stress from AI disruption fears affecting SaaS loan portfolios. Broader asset manager contagion risk.

Oil prices fell 5-6% on Iran ceasefire hopes with Brent dropping below $99. Energy sector ETF should decline as geopolitical premium gets removed despite ongoing supply disruptions. Trump's diplomatic statements reducing war premium.
